Quality Education.reason: The course is a doctoral-level program focused on advanced academic research and critical thinking in international relations, directly contributing to quality tertiary education and developing skills for global understanding (Targets 4.3, 4.4, 4.7). Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course's central theme is 'war and peace, conflict and cooperation' and the role of 'international regimes,' directly aligning with reducing violence (Target 16.1), promoting rule of law (Target 16.3), and developing effective institutions (Target 16.6).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course explicitly examines 'interdependence,' 'cooperation,' and 'international regimes,' which are core components of global partnerships, policy coherence, and international cooperation (Targets 17.6, 17.14, 17.16).
